sections.context: Warren V2 (warren/ROADMAP R-01) is making seeds the source of truth for issues — there will be no parallel warren issues table. Warren needs to attach runtime metadata to seeds (assigned agent role, scheduledFor timestamp, trigger source, last-run pointer, queued-but-don't-dispatch flag) and seeds today has fixed fields plus labels, with no structured extension point. Labels can fake `role:refactor-bot` or `trigger:cron` but lose their structure on read, can't carry timestamps, and don't round-trip cleanly. Other ecosystem tools (greenhouse, overstory) will eventually want similar runtime annotations. The persistence rule is `DB only holds runtime state; definitions, expertise, issues all live as git-tracked files in the project repo` — so seeds needs a clean place for warren-specific metadata that travels with the seed and merges cleanly across branches.
sections.approach: Add a single optional, opaque-to-seeds field `extensions?: Record<string, unknown>` to the `Issue` type. Each consumer (warren, greenhouse, overstory) owns the shape of its own namespaced keys; seeds treats the value as opaque JSON, performs no schema validation, and lets it round-trip through `.seeds/issues.jsonl` like any other field. Surface it on output (`sd show` adds a human-readable Extensions line; JSON modes already serialize the whole issue) and add a single write path on `sd update --extensions <json>` with shallow merge semantics so partial updates from different consumers do not clobber each other. Add a single, opt-in `sd ready --respect-schedule` consumer of two well-known keys (`extensions.queued`, `extensions.scheduledFor`) so warren's cron use case works on day one without changing default semantics for everyone else. This matches the established `new-issue-field-checklist` pattern (mx-c9a8e4): types.ts → output.ts → update.ts → docs, plus doctor.ts when the field has structural constraints.

alternatives.name: Inline first-class fields on Issue (scheduledFor, role, queued, lastRunId, lastRunAt at the top level)
alternatives.rejected_because: Leaks consumer-specific concerns into seeds core, locks the schema to warren's vocabulary, and provides no path for greenhouse/overstory to add their own keys without further first-class fields. Fails the seeds principle of staying minimal and consumer-agnostic.

alternatives.name: Sidecar file (.seeds/runtime.jsonl) keyed by issue id
alternatives.rejected_because: Violates the 'JSONL file IS the database' invariant — introduces a parallel store, a sync step, and the same merge-conflict surface that we already solved for issues.jsonl via `merge=union` + dedup-on-read. Doubles the lock surface for any cross-cutting write.

alternatives.name: Encode metadata in labels (role:refactor-bot, trigger:cron, sched:2026-05-12T03:00:00Z)
alternatives.rejected_because: Lossy round-trip: a label is a flat lowercased string with no structured types. Cannot carry ISO8601 timestamps as parsable values, cannot represent booleans, and consumer code has to re-parse colon-prefixed strings on every read. Conflates runtime state with discoverability tags.

alternatives.name: Per-consumer top-level keys on Issue (Issue.warren, Issue.greenhouse, Issue.overstory)
alternatives.rejected_because: Hardcodes the consumer set into seeds; every new tool requires a seeds release. A single opaque `extensions` map gets the same isolation through namespaced keys without the coupling.

steps.title: Add Issue.extensions field + JSONL round-trip test
steps.description: Phase 1 (foundation). Add `extensions?: Record<string, unknown>;` to the `Issue` interface in src/types.ts (alongside other optional fields, before the timestamp block). No changes needed to src/store.ts — append/write/read paths use JSON.stringify/JSON.parse on the whole record, so the field round-trips automatically. Add a focused test in src/store.test.ts that writes an issue with extensions = { role: 'refactor-bot', scheduledFor: '2026-05-12T03:00:00Z', queued: true, nested: { a: 1 } }, reads it back, and asserts deep equality. Add a second test that writes an issue WITHOUT extensions and confirms reading a record with no extensions key returns issue.extensions === undefined (never throws). Acceptance: bun test src/store.test.ts passes; bun run typecheck passes; the field is present on the type and survives a write-read-write cycle.

steps.title: Render extensions in sd show output
steps.description: Phase 2 (display). In src/output.ts formatIssueFull, after the Labels line and before the description, append an `Extensions:` line when issue.extensions is a non-empty object. Format as space-separated key=value pairs at the top level: scalars (string/number/boolean) are stringified directly; objects render as [obj]; arrays as [array(n)]; null as null. Keep it on a single line — this is a summary, not a dump. JSON modes (--format json) already serialize the whole issue, so no change needed in show.ts/list.ts/ready.ts JSON branches; verify that path with a test. Add a test in src/commands/show.test.ts (create if it doesn't exist) that calls run(['<id>']) on an issue with extensions and asserts the rendered string contains 'Extensions:' and the expected key=value summary. Watch out for the plain-format path in show.ts — it strips ANSI, so the Extensions line must work after stripAnsi as well.

steps.title: Add sd update --extensions <json> with shallow merge semantics
steps.description: Phase 3 (write surface). In src/commands/update.ts: register `--extensions <json>` (and matching commander option). Parse the value with JSON.parse; require it to be a plain object (not array, not null, not primitive) — throw a clear error otherwise. Merge top-level keys into the existing issue.extensions with spread: `patch.extensions = { ...(issue.extensions ?? {}), ...parsed }`. Do NOT deep-merge nested objects — V1 is shallow on purpose; document this. Also accept `--clear-extensions` boolean to set extensions to undefined (drops the field). The merge happens inside the existing `inner` closure under the issues.jsonl lock; no plan-lifecycle interaction. Add tests in src/commands/update.test.ts (create the file if it doesn't exist; follow the colocated real-I/O pattern from other *.test.ts files): (a) merging a new key preserves existing keys; (b) merging an existing key overwrites just that key; (c) --clear-extensions drops the field; (d) --extensions with a JSON array errors out; (e) --extensions with malformed JSON errors out with a useful message. Do NOT change `sd create` in this step — defer create-time extensions to a follow-up.

steps.title: Add opt-in sd ready --respect-schedule flag
steps.description: Phase 4 (consumer surface). Extend src/filter.ts with `respectSchedule?: boolean` on IssueFilterOptions, and inside applyIssueFilters when the flag is true, exclude any issue where `extensions.queued === true` OR `extensions.scheduledFor` is a string that ISO-parses to a future Date relative to Date.now(). Robust to missing/malformed values: any non-string scheduledFor is ignored (not future). Wire the flag through filterOptionsFromFlags (read flags['respect-schedule']) and through src/commands/ready.ts (register `--respect-schedule` on the commander command, push it through to the args/flags pipeline). Default behavior MUST be unchanged: without the flag, ready ignores extensions completely. Tests in src/filter.test.ts (create if missing) or src/commands/ready.test.ts: (a) without flag, queued/scheduled-future issues remain in ready output; (b) with flag, queued===true is excluded; (c) with flag, scheduledFor in the future is excluded; (d) with flag, scheduledFor in the past is INCLUDED (it's already due); (e) malformed scheduledFor doesn't throw and doesn't filter. Do NOT touch sd list — only sd ready learns this flag in V1.

steps.title: Doctor schema check for malformed extensions
steps.description: Phase 5 (integrity). In src/commands/doctor.ts, add a new check named `extensions-schema` that flags any issue where `extensions` is present and is NOT a plain object (e.g., array, primitive, null). Place it next to the existing `label-schema` check; mirror its structure. Implement the matching --fix branch that drops the malformed extensions field (sets it to undefined). Update the hardcoded pass count in doctor.test.ts (mx-957e8f convention — bumping the doctor check count requires updating the expected-pass count in the test). Add focused tests for: malformed cases (extensions = []), passing case (extensions = {role:'x'}), and --fix behavior.

steps.title: Update README, CHANGELOG, CLAUDE.md to document extensions
steps.description: Phase 6 (docs). Update README.md: in the data model section, document Issue.extensions as an opaque consumer-owned bag; document the warren namespacing convention (top-level keys: role, scheduledFor, trigger, queued, lastRunId, lastRunAt) as an example, not a contract. Document `sd update --extensions <json>` (shallow merge) and `--clear-extensions`. Document `sd ready --respect-schedule` and the two well-known keys it reads. Update CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] with a `### Added` entry for Issue.extensions, sd update --extensions, sd ready --respect-schedule, and the doctor extensions-schema check. Update CLAUDE.md (project root) data model section to list the new field. Bump src/commands/onboard.ts ONBOARD_VERSION (mx-c97ad9 convention) only if the new field is mentioned in the onboard snippet — otherwise leave it. Run `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` from seeds/ as the final gate. Do NOT bump the package version in this step — release is a separate /release flow.

risks: Future first-class field collision: if seeds later promotes (e.g.) scheduledFor to a top-level Issue field, it will collide with consumer-namespaced extensions.scheduledFor. Mitigation: document the warren keys as a convention pinned to extensions.* — promotion to first-class would require a migration. Out of scope for this plan.

Shallow-merge surprises: a consumer expecting deep-merge for nested objects (e.g., extensions.lastRun.id vs extensions.lastRun.at) will overwrite the entire nested value when patching. Mitigation: document shallow semantics explicitly; consumers that need deeper structure should keep keys flat at the top level (lastRunId, lastRunAt) per the warren example.

Default-behavior drift on sd ready: if --respect-schedule were on by default, agents would silently lose visibility into queued items. Mitigation: opt-in flag for V1 (per the seed's open question); revisit default behavior only after warren ships and we have real usage data.

JSONL union-merge across branches with concurrent extension writes: two branches merging `extensions: {role:'a'}` and `extensions: {scheduledFor:'...'}` will produce two issue records that dedup-on-read collapses to last-occurrence-wins, losing one branch's update. Mitigation: same as today for label edits — acceptable per the existing merge model; document it. Consumers requiring lock-step writes should serialize through a single agent.

Doctor false-positives if a consumer ever writes a JSON array intentionally: we explicitly reject arrays at the top level to keep the merge model coherent. Mitigation: document `extensions must be a plain object` in README; this is a deliberate constraint, not a bug.
acceptance: Issue.extensions?: Record<string, unknown> declared in src/types.ts; bun run typecheck passes.

Round-trip test in src/store.test.ts: write an issue with non-trivial extensions (mix of scalars, ISO8601 strings, nested object), read it back, assert deep equality. Confirms the field is preserved byte-for-byte through .seeds/issues.jsonl.

sd update <id> --extensions '{"role":"refactor-bot"}' shallow-merges into existing extensions (other keys preserved). --clear-extensions removes the field. Malformed JSON or non-object input fails with a clear error. Covered by tests in src/commands/update.test.ts.
sd show <id> displays an Extensions: <key=value ...> line when present (default + plain formats). --format json includes extensions on the issue payload.
sd list --format json and sd ready --format json include extensions on each issue (no code change required; verify in a smoke test).
sd ready --respect-schedule excludes issues where extensions.queued === true or extensions.scheduledFor parses to a future Date. Default sd ready (no flag) is byte-identical to today.
sd doctor includes an extensions-schema check that flags non-object extensions; sd doctor --fix drops malformed values. Doctor test pass count is updated.
README.md, CHANGELOG.md ([Unreleased]), and root CLAUDE.md document the new field, the warren namespacing convention (as example), the shallow-merge contract, and the --respect-schedule flag.
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass from seeds/.

sections.context: sd plan submit always spawns brand-new child seeds from steps[]. There is no way to adopt an already-open seed into a new or existing plan, nor to detach a seed from a plan without closing it. The seed↔plan link is just three Issue fields (plan_id, plan_step_index, requires_plan) plus the blocks/blockedBy edges between parent seed and children. Backref text in description is managed by src/plan-backref.ts:applyPlanBackref(), which already preserves surrounding content. The closest existing analogue is applyOverwrite (src/commands/plan.ts:751-947), which matches old children to new steps by title; we extend this with a more reliable match-by-seed-id path. Need bidirectional linking so agents can stage related open issues into a coherent plan instead of duplicating them.
sections.approach: Add three surfaces, all additive to the existing data model: (1) extend the submitted Step JSON schema with an optional existing_seed: string field so a step at submit time either adopts the named open seed or spawns a fresh child (today's behavior); (2) add `sd plan adopt <plan-id> <seed-id...> [--step <i>]` for post-submit adoption; (3) add `sd plan release <plan-id> <seed-id...>` as the inverse (detach without closing). Reuse applyPlanBackref for description handling and existing lock order (outer plansPath, inner issuesPath, mx-f29e43). Adoption is link-only: don't mutate status, assignee, labels, priority — those stay with the seed. Release strips the backref block via the seeds:plan-backref markers, clears plan_id + plan_step_index, and removes the parent seed↔child edges. The overwrite path is extended to match by existing_seed id first, then by step.title (preserves today's behavior for plans that don't use adoption).

alternatives.name: Adoption via sd update only (no plan-aware command)
alternatives.rejected_because: Setting plan_id with `sd update --extensions` is opaque and doesn't wire blocks/blockedBy edges or apply the backref block. Plans would have inconsistent children lists.

alternatives.name: Single sd plan add/remove command instead of adopt + release
alternatives.rejected_because: Verbs matter for agent ergonomics — 'adopt' and 'release' convey the link-only contract; 'add/remove' suggests creation/deletion.

alternatives.name: Always auto-adopt seeds whose title matches a step
alternatives.rejected_because: Title collisions are common and silent adoption is dangerous. Explicit existing_seed id is the safe contract.

steps.title: Extend Step JSON schema with existing_seed in src/plan-schema.ts
Wire existing_seed into fresh-submit path in runSubmit (src/commands/plan.ts)
Update applyOverwrite to match by existing_seed id first, then by title
Add sd plan adopt <plan-id> <seed-id...> [--step <i>] subcommand
Add sd plan release <plan-id> <seed-id...> subcommand
Tag adopted children with (adopted) marker in sd plan show human output
Update PLAN_SPEC.md to document adoption + release semantics, edge cases, lock order
Update README.md + CHANGELOG.md with new commands, JSON shape, and an end-to-end adoption example
Run quality gates: bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck

risks: Adopting a seed that already has plan_id silently corrupts the source plan's children list — must reject without an explicit --reassign-from <pl> flag (deferred to a followup).

Overwrite path: if a step lists existing_seed but its id is already in plan.children as a fresh-spawned title-matched child, the matching precedence matters. Match by id first prevents this; tests must cover the precedence explicitly.
Release: stripping the backref block must use the exact seeds:plan-backref markers — string-based search/replace will eat user-edited content otherwise.
Title mismatch on adoption: seed.title wins over step.title. Agents writing plans by hand may be surprised; stderr warning is required.
acceptance: sd plan submit accepts a step shape with existing_seed; the named seed is linked into the plan without a new seed being created.
sd plan adopt <plan-id> <seed-id...> attaches the seeds, applies backrefs, wires edges, appends to plan.children, and bumps plan.revision.
sd plan release <plan-id> <seed-id...> detaches the seeds, strips backrefs, unwires edges, removes from plan.children, and bumps plan.revision; the seed itself remains open.
Adopting a closed seed is rejected with a clear error; adopting a seed that already has plan_id is rejected unless explicitly reassigned (out of scope here — reject for now).
applyOverwrite matches existing children by existing_seed id first, then by step.title; pre-existing overwrite-by-title behavior is unchanged when no step uses existing_seed.
sd plan show distinguishes adopted children from spawned children in human output (e.g. 'seeds-abcd (adopted)').
PLAN_SPEC.md documents the adoption + release lifecycle, the schema addition, and the edge-case decisions.
All quality gates pass: bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck.

name: Adopt and release existing seeds in plans

sections.context: Seeds integration with pi (the coding-agent harness becoming the daily-driver runtime) is conventional today: the seeds onboarding snippet in CLAUDE.md tells the LLM to run `sd prime`, `sd ready`, `sd close` and we hope it remembers. It skips `sd close` on completion, never claims issues with `sd update --status in_progress`, and parses human output when it does shell out instead of using `--json`. A pi extension hard-wires the ritual into pi lifecycle events — session_start prime + status widget, structured `sd_*` tools so the LLM stops re-parsing human output, `#sd-*` autocomplete + reference expansion so issue IDs round-trip naturally in chat, and a cached ready list invalidated on writes. Investigation in os-eco/docs/pi-extensions-investigation.md §2; pi extension API surface validated against /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.74.0. The recently-closed seeds-e445 (commit 3187ba1) added typed sections to `sd prime --json` (`closeProtocol`, `rules`, `commandGroups`, `workflows`), so this extension can render structured panels and budget injection per-section instead of shoveling the markdown blob.
sections.approach: Ship inside @os-eco/seeds-cli (Option A from the investigation doc) — adds extensions/pi/index.ts + tools.ts + autocomplete.ts + lib/ files, plus a `pi` manifest, the `pi-package` keyword, and `peerDependencies` + `peerDependenciesMeta.optional: true` in package.json. Mirror mulch's distribution shape exactly (pl-5563) so the two extensions feel like siblings. Add a new `sd setup` command for parity with `ml setup` / future `cn setup` — `sd setup pi` becomes the first recipe, writes `.pi/settings.json` idempotently, validates `pi` is on PATH, and flips the onboarding marker variant. Config knobs live under a `pi.*` namespace inside `.seeds/config.yaml` via a new `pi` block in `src/config-schema.ts` (one source of truth, file-locked atomic writes, AJV-validated; warren's UI inherits it for free). Auto-prime consumes the new typed `sections` from `sd prime --json` (not the legacy `content` blob) so the extension can selectively inject `rules` + `closeProtocol` always and budget-gate `commandGroups` / `workflows`. The onboarding marker stays on the existing three-marker scheme (`<!-- seeds:start -->`, `<!-- seeds-onboard-schema:N -->`, `<!-- seeds-onboard:v<ver> -->`) — bump `ONBOARD_SCHEMA` from 4 to 5 and emit a `:pi` suffix on the schema marker when the recipe is installed, so existing outdated-detection logic doubles as install-state detection. The seven `sd_*` custom tools are thin shims over `sd <cmd> --json`; cache invalidation watches `.seeds/issues.jsonl` mtime (cheap, no JSONL diffing) and refreshes the autocomplete list on `agent_end` only when it changed.

alternatives.name: Inject the legacy `sd prime --json` `content` blob into systemPrompt
alternatives.rejected_because: seeds-e445 just landed typed sections precisely so consumers stop treating prime as opaque markdown. Using `content` would mean re-parsing markdown headings to drive any TUI panel and would burn the budget on sections the extension already knows are low-value (e.g. workflows during a short focused session). Sections-driven injection costs nothing extra and lets `pi.prime.sections` config gate what gets injected.

alternatives.name: Add `sd onboard --with-pi` instead of a new `sd setup` command
alternatives.rejected_because: mulch and (soon) canopy both have a `setup` command with per-runtime recipes (claude/cursor/codex/pi). Bolting pi into `sd onboard` diverges from that pattern and forces every future runtime recipe (sapling, overstory) into the same overloaded command. A dedicated `sd setup` is a small file, parallel structure across the ecosystem, and idiomatic for `--check` / `--remove` subflags. The onboarding marker logic stays in onboard.ts — setup calls into it.

alternatives.name: Sibling workspace package via bun workspaces (Option B from investigation doc)
alternatives.rejected_because: Version-locking with the CLI is the whole point. The extension is a thin shim over `sd ... --json`; if the CLI changes a JSON shape, the extension must change in the same PR. One tarball, one version, cannot drift. Option B is reserved for warren in the investigation doc.

alternatives.name: New `<!-- seeds-pi-onboard:v -->` marker parallel to the existing three
alternatives.rejected_because: Doubles detection logic and forces a migration. A `:pi` suffix on the existing schema marker (and an `ONBOARD_SCHEMA` bump) is a one-line change to onboard.ts, reuses `detectStatus`, and means a single source of truth for install state. Same idiom mulch chose in pl-5563.

alternatives.name: Per-tool-call write detection (track which `sd_*` tool ran)
alternatives.rejected_because: Misses writes from bash `sd close`, hand edits to .seeds/issues.jsonl, and merges from sibling worktrees. Stat-ing `.seeds/issues.jsonl` mtime on `agent_end` is one syscall, catches everything, and matches how seeds' own `sd sync` detects pending changes.

alternatives.name: Ship sd_label and sd_plan_* tools in V1
alternatives.rejected_because: Label management is rare enough that bash `sd label add <id> <label>` is fine for V1. Plan tools (sd_plan_prompt / sd_plan_submit / sd_plan_show) carry the multi-step plan JSON contract; wrapping them as tools deserves its own design pass to decide whether the LLM should be authoring plan JSON via tool args or via a slash-command-driven editor. Deferred to v0.2 — the V1 tool surface is the core CRUD + dep + search.

alternatives.name: Shadow config in .pi/seeds.json
alternatives.rejected_because: Would lose file-locking, schema validation, and multi-agent concurrency safety that `.seeds/config.yaml` already provides. `pi.*` namespace in the existing config inherits all of it, and warren's schema-driven config UI (R-10) gets pi knobs for free.

steps.title: Foundation: extension skeleton, peer deps, pi.* config schema, sd setup scaffolding
steps.description: Create `extensions/pi/` directory with index.ts, tools.ts, autocomplete.ts, lib/. Add `pi` manifest, `pi-package` keyword, `files: ["src", "extensions"]`, and `peerDependencies` + `peerDependenciesMeta.optional: true` for `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` and `typebox` to package.json — mirror mulch's pl-5563 step 1 exactly. Add a `pi` block to `src/config-schema.ts` covering `pi.auto_prime` (bool), `pi.status_widget` (bool), `pi.prime.sections` (array of section names to inject), `pi.cache.invalidate_on_write` (bool), with sensible defaults and titles for warren's UI. Bump `config.test.ts` golden snapshot. Add `src/commands/setup.ts` + `setup.test.ts` with the command scaffolding (parses `--check`, `--remove`, dispatches to a recipe map) but no recipes registered yet — the `pi` recipe lands in step 6. Wire `setup` into `src/index.ts` command router and `sd prime` command list.

steps.title: Session_start hooks: auto-prime + status widget + agent_end cache invalidation
steps.description: On `session_start` (events: startup | reload | new | resume | fork), shell `sd prime --json` and cache the parsed `sections` object. In `before_agent_start`, append a rendered subset (per `pi.prime.sections` config — default `["closeProtocol", "rules"]`) to `event.systemPrompt`. System-prompt edits chain — append, never replace. Preload `sd ready --json` + `sd stats --json` and set the status widget via `ctx.ui.setStatus` to `sd: <n> ready / <n> in-progress / <n> blocked`. On `agent_end`, stat `<ctx.cwd>/.seeds/issues.jsonl` mtime; if changed since session_start (or last refresh), re-run `sd ready --json` + `sd stats --json` and update the widget. Persist the cached ready list via `pi.appendEntry("seeds-ready", { ids, refreshedAt })` so `/reload` restores from `ctx.sessionManager.getEntries()`.

steps.title: Custom tools: sd_create, sd_ready, sd_show, sd_update, sd_close, sd_dep, sd_search
steps.description: Register seven custom tools via `pi.registerTool` inside `session_start`. Each is a thin shim over `sd <cmd> --json`, parsing the response and returning structured results to the LLM. Tool definitions live in `extensions/pi/tools.ts` with typebox schemas. `sd_create({ title, type?, priority?, description?, assignee? })`, `sd_ready({ type?, assignee?, label?, priority_max?, limit? })`, `sd_show(id)`, `sd_update(id, { status?, priority?, assignee?, extensions? })`, `sd_close(id, { reason? })`, `sd_dep({ action: "add"|"remove"|"list", issue, depends_on? })`, `sd_search({ query, status?, type?, limit? })`. Each tool sets `promptGuidelines` telling the LLM to prefer the tool over bash for that operation. Worktree-aware: all shellouts use `ctx.cwd`, not `process.cwd()`. After any write tool fires, invalidate the cached ready list immediately (don't wait for `agent_end`).

steps.title: Autocomplete: #sd-* completion provider + input-event reference expansion
steps.description: Port pi's `github-issue-autocomplete.ts` to `extensions/pi/autocomplete.ts`. Register an autocomplete provider triggered on `#` followed by `sd-` token. Source: the cached `sd list --json` (preloaded once per session, refreshed when the ready cache invalidates). Match strategy: prefix match on id first (`#sd-1a2b…`), then fuzzy on title. On `input` event, scan the message for `#sd-<4hex>` references; for each match, shell `sd show <id> --json` and append the result as an inline blob the LLM sees but the user doesn't (configurable via `pi.reference_expansion`). Cache hits within the same input event so repeated references in one message hit one shellout.

steps.title: Commands: /sd, /sd:ready, /sd:create, /sd:show, /sd:close, /sd:claim + currentIssueId state
steps.description: Register six slash commands. `/sd` prints a short reference (mirrors `sd prime` top bullets). `/sd:ready` renders the cached ready list in a TUI panel via `ctx.ui.custom`. `/sd:create` walks `ctx.ui.input` (title) → `ctx.ui.select` (type from enum, priority from enum) → confirm. `/sd:show <id>` renders `sd show <id> --json` in a panel. `/sd:close <id>` confirms then shells `sd close <id>`. `/sd:claim <id>` runs `sd update <id> --status in_progress`, sets `currentIssueId` in extension state via `pi.appendEntry("seeds-state", { currentIssueId })`, and updates the status widget to prepend `working: <id>`. Autocompletion on `<id>` args uses the same cached list. Commands import from a `commands.ts` module, registered inside `session_start`.

steps.title: sd setup pi recipe + pi-aware onboarding marker variant
steps.description: Register the `pi` recipe inside `src/commands/setup.ts`. `sd setup pi` writes `.pi/settings.json` (creates or merges idempotently) adding the `@os-eco/seeds-cli` package entry, drops a default `.pi/seeds.json` with `enabled: true`, and updates the CLAUDE.md onboarding marker. `sd setup pi --check` returns `not_installed | up_to_date | outdated`. `sd setup pi --remove` reverses both changes. Bump `ONBOARD_SCHEMA` from 4 to 5 in `src/commands/onboard.ts`. Emit a `:pi` suffix on the schema marker (`<!-- seeds-onboard-schema:5:pi -->`) when the pi recipe is installed; emit the bare marker otherwise. Snippet body becomes a short pi-aware variant when installed (drops the `sd prime` instruction and the manual-quick-reference because the extension auto-injects them) and stays the current full snippet otherwise. `onboard.ts` reads install state from the marker suffix; setup.ts writes it.

steps.title: Tests + README
steps.description: Unit tests against a mock `ExtensionAPI` covering: session_start prime caching, before_agent_start systemPrompt append, status widget format, all seven tools (happy path + worktree resolution + error surface from non-zero `sd` exit), `#sd-*` autocomplete (prefix + fuzzy), input-event reference expansion (single ref, multi-ref dedup, no-match no-op), agent_end mtime detection + ready refresh, currentIssueId persistence across reload, and setup.ts (`pi` recipe write/check/remove idempotency + marker suffix toggle + ONBOARD_SCHEMA bump migration). Quality gates green: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. Write `extensions/pi/README.md` covering install (`pi install npm:@os-eco/seeds-cli` or `sd setup pi`), config (every `pi.*` key with defaults), tools (one-line per), commands (one-line per), autocomplete behavior, and troubleshooting (peer-dep optional warning, worktree behavior, double-prime if marker not migrated).

risks: Pi extension API drift — pinned to ^0.74.0; minor bumps may add or remove hook fields. Watch for breaking changes in session_start event shape, registerTool definition, sendMessage delivery modes, and the autocomplete provider contract. Same risk mulch's pl-5563 calls out.

Prime sections shape drift — `sd prime --json` sections (closeProtocol/rules/commandGroups/workflows) just landed in commit 3187ba1 and have no golden snapshot test guarding consumer-side stability. If section names or shapes change, auto-prime breaks silently — the systemPrompt append would emit a partial blob. Add a defensive parser in extensions/pi/lib/prime.ts that falls back to the legacy `content` field if `sections` is missing or malformed, and file a follow-up seed for a consumer-contract test.

peerDependenciesMeta.optional behavior — users who `npm install -g @os-eco/seeds-cli` for the CLI alone must not see peer-dep warnings about `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` or `typebox`. Verify with a clean install before merging step 1; same shape mulch pl-5563 step 1 validates.

Cache invalidation race with sibling worktrees / merges — mtime check on `.seeds/issues.jsonl` catches local writes and most external writes, but a `git merge` that fast-forwards while pi is idle won't trigger `agent_end`. The cached ready list will be stale until the next user prompt. Acceptable for V1; document in README and offer a `/sd:refresh` command if step 5's command surface has slack. Stronger fix is a filesystem watcher inside `session_start` — explicitly deferred.

Onboarding marker migration breakage — bumping `ONBOARD_SCHEMA` 4 → 5 marks every existing seeds-onboarded project as `outdated` until they re-run `sd onboard`. This is the intended migration path but it's a user-visible churn pulse. Make sure the snippet body change is meaningful (i.e. the pi-aware variant is genuinely shorter when installed) — otherwise we're bumping for nothing. Document the migration in the seeds CHANGELOG.

Reference expansion budget — `#sd-*` reference expansion shells `sd show --json` per unique reference and inlines the result. A user pasting a list of 20 IDs spawns 20 subprocesses and 20 blobs in the LLM context. Cap at a configurable `pi.reference_expansion.max_refs` (default 5) and surface a status-line warning when truncated. Configurable so warren-driven heavy-context sessions can raise it.

sd setup as new command surface — adding `sd setup` introduces a new top-level command needing entries in: command router (src/index.ts), `sd prime` command groups (the new command fits under agentIntegration), shell completions (completions.ts), and the `sd --help` listing. Easy to miss one and ship a half-wired command. Step 1 must touch all four; verify via the completions.test.ts golden file.
acceptance: `pi -e ./extensions/pi/index.ts` loads cleanly in this repo with no errors and no peer-dep warnings on `npm install -g @os-eco/seeds-cli`
After `session_start`, `ctx.getSystemPrompt()` contains the rendered `closeProtocol` + `rules` sections from `sd prime --json`, in that order, when `pi.auto_prime` is true
`pi.prime.sections = ["rules"]` set via `sd config set pi.prime.sections '["rules"]'` causes only rules to inject on the next session_start
Status widget reads `sd: <n> ready / <n> in-progress / <n> blocked` after `session_start` and refreshes within 1s of `agent_end` when `.seeds/issues.jsonl` mtime changed

All seven custom tools (sd_create, sd_ready, sd_show, sd_update, sd_close, sd_dep, sd_search) are registered, visible in pi's tool list, and a happy-path call to each returns structured JSON the LLM can read without re-parsing
`sd_close` with a non-existent id returns a structured error including the underlying `sd` stderr, not a thrown exception
Typing `#sd-` in the input opens an autocomplete list of cached ready ids; selecting one inserts the full `#sd-<id>` token

Sending a message containing `#sd-<id>` causes the extension to inline `sd show <id> --json` as a hidden context blob, capped at `pi.reference_expansion.max_refs` (default 5) unique references per message
`/sd:claim <id>` sets `sd update <id> --status in_progress` and updates the status widget to prepend `working: <id>`; the prefix survives `/reload`
`sd setup pi` writes `.pi/settings.json` idempotently (second run is a no-op), updates the CLAUDE.md schema marker to `:pi` suffix, and `sd setup pi --check` returns `up_to_date`
`sd setup pi --remove` reverses both changes; `sd setup pi --check` returns `not_installed`
Snippet body in CLAUDE.md is the short pi-aware variant when the `:pi` suffix is present; bare snippet otherwise
Inside a `git worktree`, all `sd_*` tools and shellouts resolve `.seeds/` via `ctx.cwd` and operate on the main repo's issues, matching `sd` CLI behavior

`sd config schema` includes the new `pi` block with `auto_prime`, `status_widget`, `prime.sections`, `cache.invalidate_on_write`, and `reference_expansion.max_refs` properties, each with title + default; `config.test.ts` golden snapshot updated
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all green
`extensions/pi/README.md` exists with install, config, tools, commands, autocomplete, and troubleshooting sections

sections.context:
Agent dogfood audit of the newly-shipped @os-eco/pi-seeds extension (commit at HEAD on 2026-05-15) surfaced 12 findings. The extension is functional and the test suite is green (822 pass), but the surface that agents actually touch has rough edges that will cause friction the moment real LLM sessions hit it:

• Doc/CLI mismatches that break the very first thing an agent tries (`sd config show pi` errors).
• Tool-surface gaps where the close-protocol prime injection tells the LLM to run rituals that have no first-class tool (e.g. `sd sync`).
• Single-id tools where the CLI is plural-aware (`sd_close`, `sd_show`).
• Lifecycle quirks (silent no-op slash commands, stale status widget, `closeProtocol` hard-coding `bun test` in every project regardless of stack).
• Reference-expansion regex that won't match the natural `#sd-<4hex>` shorthand an LLM is most likely to emit.

Left unaddressed, each one degrades the trust agents place in the extension — a silent no-op slash command or a tool that takes a single id when the LLM passed three is the kind of thing that quietly trains the model to fall back to raw Bash, defeating the whole point of shipping structured tools.
sections.approach:
Land 12 small, independently-shippable child seeds, one per finding. Group by area so reviewer context stays tight:

• 3 children touch `src/commands/config.ts` (positional path, unset GC, unset-path return shape) — same file, near-trivial edits, sequenced to avoid rebase pain.
• 2 children update agent-facing prose (`sd prime`'s default closeProtocol; extension README sections on autocomplete + auto-prime visibility).
• 4 children land in `extensions/pi/lib/tools.ts` (new `sd_sync` + optional `sd_list`/`sd_blocked`/`sd_stats`/`sd_label`; multi-id `sd_close`/`sd_show`; richer `sd_ready` description; `sd_update` no-fields error).
• 1 child updates `extensions/pi/lib/commands.ts` (notify when commands disabled).
• 1 child updates `extensions/pi/lib/autocomplete.ts` (shorthand reference regex + README note).
• 1 child updates `extensions/pi/index.ts` + `lib/status.ts` (status widget refresh on before_agent_start, or add `/sd:refresh`).

Each child is one PR, one test file delta, and shouldn't exceed ~150 LOC. Priority leans P1 (critical-path agent UX) for the bug-class findings, P2 for tool gaps, P3 for cosmetic doc edits. The umbrella seed (`seeds-045e`) tracks rollup status.

alternatives.name: Ship one mega-PR that touches every finding
alternatives.rejected_because: Twelve unrelated edits in one diff is unreviewable and rebases poorly. The seeds plan-per-child convention is specifically optimized against this — each child is independently mergeable, independently revertible, and lets us land the high-priority bug fixes (#1, #4, #6) without waiting on the cosmetic README updates.

alternatives.name: Defer the tool-surface gaps (#5, #6, #7, #8) until 'real agent usage data'
alternatives.rejected_because: Finding #6 (no `sd_sync` tool) is in the auto-injected closeProtocol every session — the gap is structural, not empirical. Waiting for data means every agent in the meantime either shells out to Bash (training the wrong reflex) or silently skips the sync step. Same with `sd_close` multi-id: the protocol literally says `sd close <id1> <id2> ...` and the tool can't satisfy that signature. Ship now, observe later.

alternatives.name: Add an `sd_*` tool for every CLI subcommand (full parity)
alternatives.rejected_because: Overkill. The audit identified specific gaps tied to the close-protocol / common workflows. Tools like `sd_doctor`, `sd_migrate-from-beads`, `sd_plan submit` are low-traffic and Bash-shelling them costs the LLM little. Keep the tool surface curated; expand if data says otherwise.

steps.title: Fix `sd config show <path>` doc/CLI mismatch — accept positional path
steps.description:
Audit finding #1. `sd config show pi` errors with 'too many arguments for show', but the README and CLAUDE.md tell agents to run exactly that. The actual syntax is `sd config show --path pi`.

**Fix:** make `sd config show` accept either `show <path>` (positional) or `show --path <path>` (current flag form). Positional wins if both are passed; emit a deprecation note if both differ.

**Files touched:** `src/commands/config.ts`, `src/commands/config.test.ts`, `extensions/pi/README.md` (if any examples need updating).

**Tests:** positional-only, flag-only, both-agree, both-disagree (positional wins).

**Quality gates:** `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. Done when an agent running `sd config show pi` gets sensible output and the README example works as written.

steps.title: Make `sd config show --path <unset>` return null instead of erroring
steps.description:
Audit finding #3. Today `sd config show --path pi` (when no `pi` block exists) returns `{success:false, error:'Path not found: pi'}` and exits 1. The error string reads like the pi binary is missing, and any tool probing optional keys has to special-case the failure. **Fix:** return `{success: true, value: null, path: '

'}` for unset paths. Keep exit 0. Reserve `success: false` for malformed paths (e.g. `..`, empty string) or genuine config-load errors. **Why:** warren V2's schema-driven config editor (per seeds CLAUDE.md) probes pi.* keys to render the UI. The current shape makes 'unset' indistinguishable from 'config file corrupt'. **Files touched:** `src/commands/config.ts`, `src/commands/config.test.ts`. Update `extensions/pi/README.md` if it documented the old shape. **Tests:** unset top-level key, unset nested key, malformed path still errors, set key returns same shape as before.

steps.title: Garbage-collect empty parent objects on `sd config unset`
steps.description:
Audit finding #2. After `sd config set pi.reference_expansion.max_refs 3` then `sd config unset pi.reference_expansion.max_refs`, the YAML retains `pi: { reference_expansion: {} }` instead of cleaning up the now-empty parents.

**Fix:** after deleting the leaf, walk up the dotted path and remove any object that has become empty. Stop walking at the root (don't delete project/version).

**Edge cases:** sibling keys at the same level (don't collapse if siblings remain), arrays (don't apply — arrays aren't navigable via dotted path anyway), comments in YAML (the parser strips them — accept that as known limitation).

**Files touched:** `src/commands/config.ts`, `src/commands/config.test.ts`.

**Tests:** unset leaf cleans up empty parent; unset leaf with sibling preserves parent; unset whole subtree (already works) still works.

steps.title: Drop Bun-specific commands from `sd prime` default closeProtocol
steps.description:
Audit finding #4. `sd prime` ships `[ ] 3. Run quality gates:          bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` as step 3 of the closeProtocol. The pi extension auto-injects this on every session_start, so agents in Python/Go/Rust seeds-tracked projects get told to run `bun test`.

**Fix:** change the default step to a stack-neutral phrasing, e.g. `Run quality gates (tests, lint, typecheck per project conventions)`. Keep the slot for project-specific overrides via a future `.seeds/config.yaml` field (e.g. `prime.close_protocol.quality_gates_command`), but don't block this child on shipping the override — just remove the false universal.

**Files touched:** `src/commands/prime.ts`, `src/commands/prime.test.ts`. Update `CLAUDE.md` rendered output if snapshot tests exist.

**Tests:** rendered output no longer contains the literal `bun test` string in the default close protocol; structured `closeProtocol.steps[2]` still describes quality gates conceptually.

steps.title: Add `sd_sync` LLM tool (and consider sd_list, sd_blocked, sd_stats, sd_label)
steps.description:
Audit finding #6. The auto-injected closeProtocol ends with `sd sync && git push`. There is no `sd_sync` tool, so the LLM either shells out via Bash (defeating the structured-tool design) or silently skips the most-mentioned ritual.

**Fix (this child):** add `sd_sync` with `{ status?: boolean, dryRun?: boolean }` schema, plus `sd_list` (parity with `sd_ready` filter flags but accepting `--status`, `--all`) and `sd_blocked` (zero args). Skip `sd_stats` / `sd_label` for this child — file follow-ups if usage data warrants.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/lib/tools.ts`, `extensions/pi/lib/tools.test.ts`, `extensions/pi/README.md` (custom tools table), `extensions/pi/index.ts` (registerSeedsTools call).

**Tests:** mirror the existing tool test pattern (sd_create / sd_ready) — exec invocation shape, --json appended, success + error paths.

**Done when:** an agent running its close-protocol checklist can call `sd_sync` as a first-class tool and gets back `{success, command:'sync', staged:[...], committed:bool}` (or whatever the CLI's actual `sd sync --json` shape is — verify before authoring the schema).

steps.title: Accept multiple ids in `sd_close` and `sd_show` tools
steps.description:
Audit finding #5. The CLI supports `sd close <id1> <id2> <id3>` and `sd show <id1> <id2>`, and the closeProtocol checklist literally reads `sd close <id1> <id2> ...`. The agent tools take a single `id: string`, so batch-close = N tool calls.

**Fix:** change the schema to accept either:
  - `id: string` (preserve back-compat), OR
  - `ids: string[]` (new, plural form)

If both are passed, prefer `ids` and concat. Forward the positional list to `sd close <ids…>` / `sd show <ids…>`. Single-id callers see no behavior change.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/lib/tools.ts` (buildCloseTool, buildShowTool, schemas), `extensions/pi/lib/tools.test.ts`, `extensions/pi/README.md`.

**Tests:** single id (back-compat), multi-id ids array, both passed (ids wins), neither passed (validation error), CLI receives positional list correctly.

steps.title: Make `sd_update` with no fields return a validation error
steps.description:
Audit finding #8. `sd_update <id>` with no other fields currently returns `success:true` and re-emits the unchanged issue. An LLM that forgot to pass the flag gets no signal that it did nothing — easy silent failure mode.

**Fix:** in `buildUpdateTool.execute`, after the optional-flag pushes, check whether `args.length` is still just `['update', id]`. If so, return a structured error `{success:false, command:'update', error:'sd_update requires at least one field to change (status, priority, title, labels, etc.)'}` before shelling out.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/lib/tools.ts`, `extensions/pi/lib/tools.test.ts`.

**Tests:** sd_update with only id → structured error; sd_update with --status alone → still works; sd_update with --set-labels '' (empty string clears) → still works (don't regress the deliberate empty-string sentinel).

steps.title: Surface plan-aware fields in sd_ready/sd_search tool descriptions
steps.description:
Audit finding #7. `sd_ready` returns rich payloads including `plan_id`, `plan_status`, `plan_children` per issue, but the tool description just says `Returns { success, issues, count }`. Agents reading the tool list won't know plan context is in there.

**Fix:** expand the `description` fields on `buildReadyTool` and `buildSearchTool` to mention that issues include plan_id / plan_status / plan_children when the issue is part of a plan, and label set when present. Same for `buildShowTool` which already mentions 'dependency status'.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/lib/tools.ts`. No code logic change; description-only edit. Probably no new tests needed, but verify `tools.test.ts` doesn't snapshot descriptions in a way that breaks.

**Done when:** the description string conveys 'plan context is available' without forcing the LLM to inspect raw output to discover it.

steps.title: Notify (not silently no-op) when slash commands disabled via pi.commands=false
steps.description:
Audit finding #9. The slash commands register at extension load and live forever; their handler stub `buildCommandDeps()` returns `undefined` when `pi.commands=false`, and `if (!deps) return` runs without notifying. The command appears in pi's autocomplete but does nothing.

**Fix:** when `buildCommandDeps()` returns undefined inside a handler, call `pi.notify` (or whatever UI primitive is available at command-invocation time — the handler has the `ui` context indirectly via `sessionUi` if the session is active) with a one-line warning: `'/sd:ready disabled — set pi.commands: true in .seeds/config.yaml'`.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/lib/commands.ts` (or `index.ts` where the deps-getter is closed over), `extensions/pi/lib/commands.test.ts`.

**Edge case:** session_start hasn't fired yet (no UI context) — fall back to a stderr-style chat message or just no-op gracefully. Test both branches.

**Done when:** toggling `pi.commands: false` and then pressing `/sd:ready` produces a visible warning instead of silence.

steps.title: Add `#sd-<4hex>` shorthand to reference regex + document
steps.description:
Audit finding #10. The reference regex `#sd-([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*-[A-Fa-f0-9]{4,})\b` requires the full `<project>-<hex>` form. So `#sd-7164` (project-implicit) doesn't expand, even though that's the natural shorthand both humans and LLMs reach for. The autocomplete inserts the full token, masking the gap for typists but not for LLM-emitted text.

**Fix:** extend the regex to also accept `#sd-<4+hex>` as a shorthand. Resolution: look up the bare hex in the local cache; if exactly one match, expand it; if zero or multiple, leave the token literal and surface a status warning. The full `#sd-<project>-<hex>` form remains canonical and gets autocompleted.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/lib/autocomplete.ts` (`REF_REGEX`, `expandSeedsReferences`, `extractSeedsReferences`), `extensions/pi/lib/autocomplete.test.ts`, `extensions/pi/README.md` (Autocomplete & reference expansion section).

**Tests:** bare 4-hex matches; bare 4-hex with ambiguous resolution emits a warning + leaves literal; full form still works; non-hex shorthand (`#sd-abcd` where abcd isn't hex) doesn't match.

steps.title: Refresh status widget on before_agent_start (not just agent_end)
steps.description:
Audit finding #11. The cache invalidation hook is on `agent_end`, so a `git pull` while pi is idle leaves the widget stale until the next agent turn completes. Better: also refresh on `before_agent_start` so the model that's about to run sees fresh counts.

**Fix:** factor the mtime-stat + refresh body out of the `agent_end` hook (it's already a small block in `extensions/pi/index.ts`) and invoke it from `before_agent_start` as well. Guard against double-refresh: if mtime hasn't changed since the last refresh, skip. The existing `lastIssuesMtimeMs` already does this.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/index.ts`. Optional: add a `/sd:refresh` slash command that forces a refresh independent of mtime (helps when the file system has the same mtime resolution as a busy sequence of writes).

**Tests:** status.test.ts covers `readStatus` already. Add an integration-shaped test: simulate two before_agent_start events with mtime unchanged → only one refresh. Mtime bumped between them → both refresh.

steps.title: Document auto-prime visibility + #sd reference gotcha in extension README
steps.description:
Audit finding #12 (and partial #1 cleanup). The default auto-prime injection (~819 chars) lands in `systemPrompt` invisibly. Agents inspecting their own system prompt will see 'Session Close Protocol' from nowhere and may misattribute it.

**Fix:** add a short subsection to `extensions/pi/README.md`:
  - 'What gets injected' — point to `sd prime --compact` for inspection, list the default sections, note `pi.prime.sections: []` as the kill switch.
  - Update the Autocomplete section to mention the `#sd-<project>-<hex>` canonical form vs the new shorthand (depends on the regex child, but the README edit can be sequenced after or merged with that step).
  - Replace any remaining `sd config show pi` examples with the positional-or-flag form once step 1 lands.

**Files touched:** `extensions/pi/README.md`. No code changes, no new tests.

**Done when:** a fresh reader of the README understands (a) what's silently injected, (b) how to inspect it, (c) the reference shorthand caveat.

risks: Changing `sd config show` to accept a positional path could collide with a hypothetical future subcommand named the same as a config path (e.g. `sd config show pi-something`). Mitigation: positional is treated as `--path` if it doesn't start with `--`; reserve future subcommands by prefixing with verbs (`sd config diff`, not `sd config pi`).

Returning `{success:true, value:null}` from `sd config show --path <unset>` is a JSON-schema-breaking change for any tool today that special-cases `success:false` to mean 'unset'. Mitigation: warren's only consumer is still in development; ship the change with a CHANGELOG note and search the ecosystem (`rg 'success.*false.*config.*Path not found'`) for callers.

Multi-id `sd_close` could be misused by an LLM batch-closing the wrong issues. Mitigation: the close_reason is shared across the batch — keep that constraint, and document that batching is intended for 'I just finished issues A, B, C' not 'close everything matching X'.

Hard-coded `bun test` removal regresses the seeds repo's own CLAUDE.md if downstream relies on the literal string. Mitigation: this repo's CLAUDE.md already has its own `Build & Test Commands` block — verify the prime injection isn't being relied on as the source of truth here.

Multiple children touch `extensions/pi/lib/tools.ts` (steps 5, 6, 7, 8). Land them serially or pre-coordinate rebases. The umbrella seed should reflect this dependency in commits even though the plan steps don't strictly require `blocks` linkage.
acceptance: All 12 child seeds closed with passing quality gates (`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`).
`sd config show pi` works as documented (no error) in a fresh `sd init` project.
`sd config show --path pi` in a project without pi.* config returns `{success:true, value:null}` with exit 0.
`sd config unset pi.<deep.path>` leaves no orphan empty objects in `.seeds/config.yaml`.
`sd prime --json` no longer contains the literal string `bun test` in the default closeProtocol steps.
`sd_sync`, `sd_list`, `sd_blocked` registered as LLM tools with passing unit tests in `extensions/pi/lib/tools.test.ts`.
`sd_close` and `sd_show` accept `ids: string[]` and forward correctly to the CLI; existing single-id `id: string` callers still work.
`sd_update` with no field flags returns `{success:false, error: '...'}` instead of a silent re-emit.
Tool descriptions for `sd_ready` and `sd_search` mention plan-aware payload fields.
Pressing `/sd:ready` with `pi.commands: false` produces a visible notification, not silence.
`#sd-<4hex>` shorthand expands to the canonical issue when unambiguous; ambiguous matches surface a warning.
Status widget refreshes on `before_agent_start` when issues.jsonl mtime has changed since last refresh.
`extensions/pi/README.md` documents auto-prime injection visibility and the `#sd` reference shorthand caveat.
Umbrella seed `seeds-045e` closed with a summary commit referencing every child.

sections.context: After submitting a plan, the only way to change sections or step metadata is sd plan submit --overwrite, which requires re-submitting the entire plan JSON from scratch. This is high-friction for common edits: fixing a typo in the approach section, renaming a step, or changing a step's priority. Users end up either living with mistakes or going through the full overwrite ceremony. An in-place edit command (sd plan edit) would let users make targeted, field-level changes with a single CLI invocation, matching the ergonomics of sd update for issues.
sections.approach: Add a new sd plan edit subcommand with field-level flags: --name for the plan label, --section <name> <text> for replacing section content, and --step <i> combined with --title/--priority/--type for step metadata. The implementation follows the pattern established by runOutcome and runReview: resolve plan id via resolvePlanIdArg, acquire outer plans lock + inner issues lock, mutate in place, bump revision, write. Step metadata edits propagate to the corresponding child seed (looked up via plan.children[stepIndex]). Approach section changes trigger a backref refresh on all child seeds (reusing applyPlanBackref). Validation is lightweight — no full template re-validation, just type/range checks on individual fields. This avoids the complexity of structural edits (add/remove/reorder steps) which would require child spawning/orphaning logic and block-index renumbering.

alternatives.name: Interactive JSON editor (sd plan edit --interactive)
alternatives.rejected_because: Seeds is a CLI tool consumed by agents and scripts. Interactive editing requires a $EDITOR flow or TUI, adds complexity, and doesn't compose well with automation. Flag-based edits are atomic, scriptable, and testable.

alternatives.name: Extend --overwrite with partial merge semantics
alternatives.rejected_because: Overwrite is designed for full plan replacement with child matching. Adding partial-merge to it would complicate an already complex function (applyOverwrite is 280 lines) and conflate two different use cases. A separate edit command keeps concerns clean.

alternatives.name: Include structural edits (add/remove/reorder steps) in V1
alternatives.rejected_because: Structural edits require spawning/orphaning children, renumbering plan_step_index on all siblings, and rewriting step.blocks indices. This is the complexity of applyOverwrite and doesn't belong in a lightweight edit command. Users who need structural changes should use --overwrite.

steps.title: Register sd plan edit subcommand and implement --name editing
Implement --section editing with approach backref refresh
Implement --step metadata editing with child-seed propagation
Update docs: CLAUDE.md, README, PLAN_SPEC, prime, onboard

risks: Approach section edits must refresh backrefs on all children — if a plan has many children this is O(n) issue writes, but plans rarely exceed ~10 steps so this is acceptable.
Step index addressing (--step <i>) uses 1-based indices to match step.blocks convention — must be consistent and well-documented to avoid off-by-one confusion.
Concurrent edits from multiple agents could race on the same plan — existing advisory lock model handles this (same as overwrite), but worth noting.
acceptance: sd plan edit <id> --name <text> updates plan.name, bumps revision, persists
sd plan edit <id> --section approach <text> replaces plan.sections.approach and refreshes backrefs on all child seeds
sd plan edit <id> --section context <text> replaces plan.sections.context without touching children
sd plan edit <id> --step 2 --title <text> renames step 2's child seed title and bumps revision
sd plan edit <id> --step 2 --priority 1 changes the child seed's priority
sd plan edit <id> --step 2 --type bug changes the child seed's type
Multiple flags in one invocation apply atomically (e.g. --name X --section approach Y)
All edits bump plan.revision and update plan.updatedAt
JSON output mode (--json) works for all edit operations
Invalid --step index (out of range) exits non-zero with clear error
Tests cover: name edit, section edit, approach backref refresh, step title/priority/type propagation, combined edits, out-of-range step, missing plan

sections.context: The seeds test suite takes ~99s for 825 tests (8.3 tests/sec). Root cause: 22 of 37 test files invoke every CLI operation via Bun.spawn(['bun', 'run', 'src/index.ts', ...]), spawning an estimated 2,000-3,000 subprocesses per run. Each subprocess pays full Bun startup + TypeScript module resolution + commander parsing overhead. The 15 unit test files that import functions directly complete in ~2.5s at 130+ tests/sec — a 25x throughput difference. The top 6 files (plan.test.ts at 38s, label 6s, create 5.8s, unblock 5.2s, list 5s, dep 4.6s) account for 65s / 65% of total runtime. Critically, every command already exports a `run(args, seedsDir)` function — the tests simply don't use them. The refactor is a test-level change: swap subprocess spawning for direct function calls with stdout/stderr capture.
sections.approach: Build a lightweight in-process test harness that captures console.log/console.error/process.stdout.write/Bun.write(Bun.stdout) output, then convert command tests to call the exported run() functions directly instead of spawning subprocesses. Commands already export run(args, seedsDir?) — no production code refactor needed. A small set of CLI smoke tests (5-10) remain subprocess-based to verify the commander wiring, --help, --version, and exit codes. The conversion is mechanical and file-by-file, so each step is independently shippable and testable.

alternatives.name: Pre-compile a binary with bun build and spawn that
alternatives.rejected_because: Reduces per-spawn overhead but doesn't eliminate it; still ~1,000+ process spawns paying OS-level fork/exec cost

alternatives.name: Use bun --preload to cache module resolution across spawns
alternatives.rejected_because: bun test doesn't support preload for spawned child processes; each subprocess still does full module resolution independently

alternatives.name: Run test files in parallel with more workers
alternatives.rejected_because: bun already runs files in parallel; the bottleneck is sequential subprocess spawns within each file, not cross-file parallelism

alternatives.name: Batch setup by sharing tmpdir across tests in a describe block
alternatives.rejected_because: Saves some beforeEach spawns but most subprocess calls are in test bodies; also couples tests and makes failures harder to isolate

steps.title: Build in-process test harness for capturing command output
steps.description: Create src/test-harness.ts: a runCommand(args, seedsDir) helper that (1) monkey-patches console.log, console.error, process.stdout.write, and Bun.write to capture output into buffers, (2) calls the command's exported run() function, (3) restores originals in a finally block, (4) returns {stdout, stderr, exitCode} matching the subprocess-based run() signature so test migration is a drop-in replacement. Handle process.exitCode reads. Export a createTestDir() helper that does mkdtemp + returns cleanup function. Export an initTestProject(dir) that calls init's run() directly.

steps.title: Convert plan.test.ts to in-process (38s → ~2-3s target)
steps.description: The single largest bottleneck: 182 tests, 38s, ~460 subprocess spawns. Replace the file-local run() helper with the test harness import. Convert beforeEach sd-init calls to use initTestProject(). Convert all Bun.spawn([...CLI...]) calls to runCommand(). Verify all 182 tests pass. This step alone should cut suite time by ~35s.

steps.title: Convert label, create, unblock, dep, list tests to in-process (~27s → ~2s target)
steps.description: The next 5 slowest files: label.test.ts (24 tests, 6s), create.test.ts (35 tests, 5.8s), unblock.test.ts (17 tests, 5.2s), list.test.ts (23 tests, 5s), dep.test.ts (16 tests, 4.6s). Same mechanical conversion: swap subprocess run() for harness runCommand(). These share an identical test structure pattern so the conversion is repetitive. Combined ~27s savings.

steps.title: Convert remaining command tests to in-process (~30s → ~2s target)
steps.description: Convert the remaining 15 command test files: doctor (4.3s), tpl (3.3s), update (3s), format (2.8s), search (2.5s), config (2.1s), ready (2.1s), onboard (2s), plan-submit-record-decision (2s), show (1.7s), setup (1.5s), plan-mulch (1.4s), sync (1s), prime (0.65s), completions (0.3s). sync.test.ts needs special handling since it also spawns git commands — keep those as real spawns. plan-submit-record-decision.test.ts spawns a fake ml binary — keep that pattern but convert the sd calls.

steps.title: Add CLI smoke test file for subprocess-level coverage
steps.description: Create src/commands/cli-smoke.test.ts with 5-10 tests that actually spawn the CLI binary. Cover: --help output, --version output, unknown command error + exit code, sd init + sd create + sd list round-trip, --json flag produces valid JSON, --quiet suppresses non-JSON output. This preserves confidence that the commander wiring and process-level behavior work correctly, without needing every test to pay subprocess cost.

steps.title: Validate full suite performance and clean up
steps.description: Run full suite, verify all 825+ tests pass, measure total time (target: <15s). Run lint + typecheck. Remove any dead subprocess helper code from converted files. Verify CI passes. Document the test harness pattern in CLAUDE.md testing section so future tests use in-process by default.

risks: Console/stdout monkey-patching may miss output paths — some commands use process.stdout.write directly or Bun.write(Bun.stdout); the harness must intercept all four output channels
Commands that set process.exitCode need careful handling — the harness must snapshot and restore exitCode around each call to avoid test pollution
Chalk color output may differ between subprocess (which detects a pipe) and in-process (which detects a TTY) — tests that assert on ANSI codes may need NO_COLOR=1 or force-color env
sync.test.ts depends on real git operations (git init, git add, git commit) — those subprocess calls must remain; only the sd CLI calls should be converted
plan-submit-record-decision.test.ts creates a fake ml binary on PATH — the PATH manipulation works in-process but needs to happen before the run() call, not before a spawn
acceptance: All existing tests pass (825+ tests, 0 failures)
Full suite completes in under 15 seconds (down from ~99s)
No subprocess spawning in command tests except cli-smoke.test.ts and explicitly justified cases (git in sync.test.ts, fake binaries in plan-mulch/record-decision tests)
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass
Test harness is documented and new tests default to in-process pattern

sections.context: Warren's patrol agents (nightwatch, bugwatch) create seeds plans to decompose their automated work. Child seeds spawned from plan steps need to carry agent-name labels (e.g. 'nightwatch', 'bugwatch') so issues are traceable to the originating agent. Today, SubmittedStep in src/commands/plan.ts has no labels field — the only way to label plan children is post-hoc sd label add calls, which is fragile and verbose in agent prompts. Adding labels at plan-step level lets agents declare labels declaratively in the plan JSON and have them flow through to child seeds at spawn time.
sections.approach: Add an optional labels: string[] field to SubmittedStep and the STEP_SCHEMA in plan-schema.ts. Reuse the same normalization logic as sd label add (lowercase, trim, dedup). In the fresh-spawn path of runSubmit, set issue.labels from the normalized step labels. In the adoption path (both submit-time existing_seed and runAdopt post-submit), merge step labels additively into the adopted seed's existing labels using Set dedup. Validate in STEP_SCHEMA that labels is an array of non-empty strings. Keep the overwrite path consistent — new steps carry labels, matched children get labels merged. This follows the established pattern of how other step fields (type, priority, plan_template) flow from step definition to child seed.

alternatives.name: Top-level plan labels inherited by all children
alternatives.rejected_because: Too coarse — different steps may need different labels (e.g. one step tagged 'nightwatch' another tagged 'debt'). Per-step labels are more flexible and match the existing per-step type/priority model.

alternatives.name: Use extensions field instead of first-class labels
alternatives.rejected_because: Labels are already a first-class Issue field with normalization, filtering (sd list --label), and dedup semantics. Using extensions would bypass all of that and require consumers to re-implement normalization.

steps.title: Add labels to STEP_SCHEMA and SubmittedStep interface
steps.description: In src/plan-schema.ts, add a 'labels' property to STEP_SCHEMA: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string', minLength: 1 } }. In src/commands/plan.ts, add 'labels?: string[]' to the SubmittedStep interface. This is schema-only — no behavioral changes yet.

steps.title: Wire labels into fresh-spawn path of runSubmit
steps.description: In runSubmit's fresh-spawn loop (both fresh-submit and overwrite paths), normalize step.labels using the same lowercase/trim/dedup logic as label.ts normalizeLabels, then set issue.labels on the spawned child Issue when labels are present and non-empty after normalization. Extract or reuse the normalizeLabels helper so it's importable from plan.ts.

steps.title: Wire labels into adoption paths (submit-time and runAdopt)
steps.description: In the adoption branch of runSubmit (existing_seed path), merge normalized step.labels additively into the adopted seed's existing labels using Set dedup — same merge semantics as sd label add. Also update the runAdopt post-submit function to accept and merge labels when the step at --step index has labels. For overwrite adoption (matched children), merge labels additively.

steps.title: Add tests for step labels across all paths
steps.description: In src/commands/plan.test.ts, add test cases: (1) fresh-spawn with labels — child seeds receive normalized labels. (2) Adoption with labels — labels merge into existing seed labels without duplicates. (3) Overwrite with labels — new step labels merge into matched children. (4) Validation rejects empty-string labels. (5) Labels normalize (uppercase→lowercase, whitespace trimmed, duplicates removed). (6) Steps without labels — children have no labels (backward compat).

steps.title: Update CLAUDE.md and sd plan prompt output to document labels field
steps.description: Update the CLAUDE.md plan command reference to mention the optional labels field on plan steps. Ensure sd plan prompt --json output includes labels in the step schema description so LLMs know the field exists when filling plan templates.

risks: normalizeLabels is currently local to label.ts — extracting it to a shared location or duplicating the logic needs to avoid import cycles
Overwrite path label merge must not clobber labels that users added manually after initial spawn — additive-only merge is critical
acceptance: sd plan submit with step labels spawns child seeds that have those labels (normalized) on the Issue
sd plan submit with step.existing_seed and step.labels merges labels additively into the adopted seed
sd plan submit --overwrite with step labels merges labels into matched children additively
STEP_SCHEMA rejects plans where a step label is an empty string after trimming
All existing plan.test.ts tests continue to pass (backward compatibility)
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass

name: Plan step labels for child seeds
